content_en:
Another way of dealing with Al-'Ayn is to try not to provoke it in the first place, by covering the beauty of the one for whom you fear with something that will repel it. 'Uthmaan ~ saw a handsome boy, and said : "Put something black on his chin, lest Al-'Ayn harm him" - meaning that they should put something black in the dimple of his chin. r631 (62] Muwatta lmaam Malik. Shaykh Muhammad As-Saalih bin Uthaymeen said: "Part of the treatment of the evil eye effect is asking the person who has put the evil eye on another to wash, as the Prophet ~ commanded 'Aamir bin Rabee'ah ~ to do in the Hadeeth quoted above. Then the water should be poured over the one who has been afflicted . With regard to taking his waste, such as his urine and stools, there is no basis for doing so; the same applies to taking any of his belongings.

Rather what is narrated is that which is mentioned above, washing his limbs and washing inside his garment, or likewise washing inside his headgear and garments. And Allaah knows best. There is nothing wrong with taking precautions against the evil eye before it happens, and this does not contradict the idea of Tawakkul (putting one's trust in Allaah) . In fact this is Tawakkul, because Tawakkul means putting one's t rust in Allaah whilst also implementing the means that have been permitted or enjoined ... " (AI-Fataawa) (63] AI-Baghawi - Sharh AI-Sunnah: 59 Treatment for Envy (A general guide) Allaah ~ grades all of His creation the way He likes Why should you be envious of another creature like yourself while it is Allaah ~ that grants excellence to any amongst them the way He desires based upon His Perfect Wisdom and Judgement.

This is the Sunnah of Allaah and in it there is no alteration. He ~ says: ~,,,, / ,,,,,,, , , . , , ~-:F >t ,,,,,,,... J// ..., , , , , , , , , ., ,. ., , ,. " " , , ., , // / ~ ~~~ ~I ~ ~ . ~ ~ .r,_ c.1;. ~ ~ I ~I / / ~ r "That has been the Way of Allaah already with those who passed away before. And you will not find any change in the Way of Allaah." [Al-Fath (48): 23] So you will find that Allaah ~ has given an edge to some over the others even among His Names!

Excellence among His Names and Attributes All of Allaah's Names and Attributes are perfect and beautiful. However, some amongst them have been labelled the "greatest names by His Messenger ~ - Buraydah ~ narrated that the Messenger of Allaah heard a man praying and he was saying: "O Allaah ! Verily I ask you by virtue of the fact that I testify that you are Allaah and there is no deity worthy of worship except you; AI-AhacJf-641, As-SamacJf65 1, He who does not beget nor was He begotten and there is no one comparable to Him." Then the Messenger of Allaah :i said : / ,,,, ,., ~ J ~G:-1 ~ ~~ "Indeed, you have asked Allaah the Most Exalted by the Names with which when He is asked, He gives, and when He is invoked He responds. " And in another narration, he said: _1;.;.\11 /.&1 t"""• ~:- 4111 ~L .Ll r- / / "Surely, you have asked Allaah by virtue of His greatest Names."f-661 [64] The Only One. (65] The Independent, the Eternal and the Absolute.

(66] Abu Dawood, Tirmidhee and An-Nasaaee. Excellence among His Books Allaah has revealed books to His Prophets and Messengers. However, He made the Qur'aan to excel all of them and He ~ has promised to protect it from any alteration. A promise not given to the earlier books. He ~ says: "Verily It is We who have sent down the Dhikr (i.e. the Qur'aan) and surely, We will guard it from corruption." [AIHijr (15) : 9] Excellence amongst His Prophets Allaah ~ compared the people for excellence, the best of them being the Prophets, and He ~ compared the Messengers for excellence, the most excellent of them being Muhammad ~ - Allaah ~ says: At a different time, Anas bin Malik ..t;, narrated that while he was seated with the Messenger of Allaah ~ and a man was offering the Sa/aah and thereafter he prayed saying: "O Allaah !

Certainly I ask you by virtue of the fact that the Praise is for you, there is no deity worthy of worship in truth except you; Al - Manaan - The Benefactor, Badee'u samaawaati wal-ard - The Originator of the Heavens and the earth, Ya Dha/-Jalaal wal-/kraam - Owner of Majesty and Honour, Yaa Hayyu Yaa Qayyuum - The Ever living one that dies not, The Self-Subsisting and Independent." So the Prophet ~ said : "He has indeed asked Allaah and invoked Him by virtue of His greatest Names, with which whenever He is invoked He responds and when He is asked by them, He gives." (Abu Dawood and An-Nasaa'ee.) 63 "Those Messengers! We preferred some to others." [AIBaqarah (2): 253]. Excellence among the Muslim Nation Allaah g compared the believers in the Ummah (Community) of Muhammad ~ for excellence, and the best of them, after His Prophet ti was Abu Bakr As-Siddeeq ~ .[671 Excellence of the children of Aadam over the rest of creation: Allaah ~ says: "And indeed we have honoured the children of Aadam, and we have carried them on land and sea, and have provided them with lawful good things, and have preferred them above many of those whom we have created with a marked preference." [Al-lsraa (17): 70] Allaah ~ compared the children of Aadam for excellence and (67) Narrated Muhammad bin Al Hanafiya I asked my father ('Alee bin Abi Taalib ~ ), "Who are the best people after Allaah's Messenger ~ ?" He said, "Abu Bakr." I asked, "Who then?" He said, "Then 'Umar." I was afraid he would say "Uthman," so I said, "Then you?" He said, "I am only an ordinary person ." (AI-Bukhaaree) Likewise lbn Umar ~ narrated that: "We used to compare the people as to who was better during the lifetime of Allaah's Messenger ~ - We used to regard Abu Bakr as the best, then 'Umar, and then 'Uthman." (AI-Bukhaaree) 64 made the people of goodness and benefits to mankind excel the others. He ~ says: ,\; _) >~- " ~ ~'_,, t ~~ ~,_,.> J":, )l::.A /// >-;; \ , u ' / / }.._ i;_r _ l 1..--- -: .i..ll Y~_J "T J:oi ~ -t '1 i.p;> <>" ,/ J ~ ..r-- .:-:>: d - LllJ-4 ;., '-::-' - ~~ '~·__,, ~ ,I._ -~'~.1,: ,'(; J1Jt;., _r4 "'Z -1 -~ ..... /J"f'; &-' Y'-' / '! .>>t _,,,, ,,, . / > / - (Y_, Y' , :'" l>r "And Allaah puts forward another example of two men, one of them dumb, who has no power over anything (disbeliever) , and he is a burden to his master, whichever way he directs him, he brings no good. Is such a man equal to one (believer) who commands justice, and is himself on the Straight Path?" [An-Nahl (16): 76].

He ~ even assigned excellence amongst the foods and plants: "And in the earth are neighbouring tracts and gardens of vines, and fields sown with corn, and palm trees, growing out two or three from a single stem root, or otherwise (one stem for every palm), watered with the same water, yet some of them We make more excellent than others to eat. Verily, in these things, there are signs for the people who understand." [Ar-Ra'd (13): 4]. 65 Excellence of the Night and Day: Allaah ~ compared the days on earth for excellence, Layla-tu/-Qadr - The Night of Decree - being better than a thousand months: He ~ says: "Layla-tul-Qadr is better than a thousand months." [AIQadr (97): 3]. And He ~ made Yawmul Jumu'ah - Friday - to excel the rest of the days of the week. Narrated Abu Hurayrah ~ - "Allaah's Messenger ~ said: 4-' ;T /-\~ W 4 ~ \ -'o/ >~{I\~ ~ i . ~ /-/ / . i y.. ~ /- i y..o/ ~ >o-; r:; ., ,.,., • .,.,.... ,,,, ., ,,,,.,, ~ 'll ~w1 f.;; '1:, 4~ t_/i ~:, 4~1 ~;i ~:, 0 ., ~ \ 0 / • / . iY..c.1- "The best day on which the sun has risen is Friday; on it Aadam ~\ was created, on it he was made to enter Paradise, on it he was expelled from it."l 68 l If you reflect carefully upon all that has been stated, you will see that Allaah is the Doer of all that He intends and that no one can question His Authority. Instead of grudging Allaah's decision, the various forms of excellence granted to different people should instill in you: a.

The absolute faith in Allaah . b. Being pleased with Qadar as you continue to strive in your endeavours. [68) Muslim. c. The ability to avoid the rivalry for excellence for the sake of th is world. This is the first and the surest cure for the di sease of envy. From the other ways of cure of this disease are: Love of One Another What every Muslim is obliged to do is to love for his brother what he loves for himself of good things, and to hate for his brother what he hates for himself of bad things.

This does not mean that you cannot like for yourself what you like for others. If you see that your brother has something that you do not possess, and wish that you have it too, this is the good envy that is free from malicel69l; however, if you wish that the blessing would be taken away from him, this is the destructive jealousy. As a Muslim, you need to strive against your own selfl 70l so that your heart will be free of jealousy towards your Muslim brothers. If you sincerely Jove your brothers, most of these problems from which you might be suffering will disappear. [69] This is the praiseworthy envy called AI-Ghibtah. A discussion on it follows shortly. [70] Jihaad AI-Nafs: Striving against the soul because it is always inclined to evil.

Allaah ~ says : Yusuf ~ said : "And I free not myself from the blame. Verily, the human self is inclined to evil, except when my Lord bestows His Mercy upon whom He wills. Verily, my Lord is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful." [Yusuf (12) : 53] 67 When you realize how great your virtue and status will be when you love your brothers and love good things for them, and when you know how great your reward will be if you treat them well, this will motivate you to treat them well in all ways, and to strive to benefit your brothers and sisters instead of being preoccupied with jealous thoughts of what they have and you do not have. You need to reflect and think deeply these words of Allaah: 1': ~ j. ,,, fi. .,,, _,.,:.1.,,, .),..,,,..... ., , / 'J ,-;,, ). ,:, ,-; / ,,,, ~ , ~ t:t'.J JJ!\_J ~~ 0-4 ~J: ~1 µ ~·~r "That (which you are envious and sad about) is the Grace of Allaah which He bestows on whom He wills. And Allaah is All-Sufficient for His creatures' needs, All-Knower." [AIMad'idah (5) :54] And also His ~ saying: "Is it they who would portion out the Mercy of your Lord?

It is We Who portion out between them their livelihood in this world, and We raised some of them above others in ranks, so that some may employ others in their work. But the Mercy {Paradise) of your Lord is better than the wealth of this world, which they amass." [Az-Zukhruf (43): 32] Piety and Patience Hasad - Destructive jealousy - causes a great deal of harm in this world and in the Hereafter. P 1l That is why "Whoever finds in himself any Hasad towards another has to try to neutralize it by means of piety and consciousness of Allaah along with patience. So he should hate the feeling of Hasad in himself... But the one who does wrong to his brother by word or deed will be punished for that.

The one who fears Allaah and is patient, however, is not included among the wrongdoers, and Allaah will benefit him by virtue of his piety and consciousness of Allaah ." (721 Supplication Make Du'aa' (supplication) to Allaah ~ and ask Him to rid you of th is problem. The Prophet ~ used to say in his Du'aa', "Guide my heart and remove ill will from my breast."f.731 Reading the Qur'aan. Ponderingthe meanings of the Qur'aan and reading it frequently, especially the Verses which speak of Hasad (destructive jealousy). Reading the Qur'aan brings you a great deal of reward for good (71] This is evident from the Hadeeth of AI-Zubayr bin AI-Awwam ~ stated on page 36. (72] Shaykh ul-lslaam lbn Taymiyyah : Amraad AI-Qu/oob (Diseases of the Heart) : (73] At-Tirmidhee.

J o ,.,. l I ·- 1/ • I/ O ,,,, O / •/ . / I; (.>.)...L..., .. ,,,, ~ r...r_J (J':"'"' .,. ,,,, ~ _J " Wa 'hdi qa/bi wa'slul sakheemata sadree'~ The phrase "guide my heart" means to the straight path, and "remove ill will from my breast" means take away all insincerity, rancour and hatred. deeds. Allaah ~ says: "Verily, the good deeds remove the evil deeds.'' [Hud (11):114] And certainly, it is in the words of Allaah that all hearts find rest and tranquility. Allaah ~ says: ~yfll ~ .&, ~~ 1t~ !::,, / ,,.,,. /;,, "Verily, in the remembrance of Allaah do hearts find rest.'' [Ar-Ra'd (13): 28] Reading the History of the Prophet and the Salafus-Saaliheen[74J Reading the SeeraH 15 l of the Messenger of Allaah ~ , seeing how he kept away from Hasad and how he loved good for others, even for his enemies. Seeking Allaah's refuge! If any such thoughts of Hasad, cross your mind, then seek refuge with Allaah from the accursed Shaytaan, and keep yourself (74] Those consisting of the companions, their successors and their righteous followers and upholders of the Sunnah from the earlier generations of Muslims.

(75] Biography. An addition to this is reading the biographies and stories of the Sahaabah. busy with something that will make you forget these insinuating whispers and thoughts. If the Shaytaan manages to instil Hasad in your heart, then beware lest you say or do anything, which will show that Hasad. Every person has his or her share of hasad. Shaykh Al-lslaam lbn Taymiyyah said: "Nobody is free from Hasad, but the noble person hides it whilst the base person shows it."[76l Remember, a person will not be brought to account for whatever crosses his mind, but he will be brought to account for what he says and does.

The Prophet ~ said: <~· I t; J/ 0r ~ I.r' ..r"-'-'-" / M .,,, / ·~11 J/ /tl;;J1 ~ M ·/ ~ / ~ ,,,, . -"½ / ., ... "Allaah will forgive my Um mah for their mistakes, what they forget and what they are forced to do."f.771 Give a gift and a handshake. If you feel that you are jealous of a specific person, then buy him a gift and shake hands with him. The Prophet ~ said : is:.ll101.:; 1;~ '1~;~_:; 'JJ1 0~ ' I ~ ~ "Shake hands, for this will dispel rancour, and exchange gifts and love one another, for this will dispel hatred."17 81 Saying Salaam Alaykum! Hasad is the result of hatred, whose opposite is love, the way [76] Amraad AI-Quloob - Diseases of the Hearts. [77] AI-Bukhaaree. [78] Narrated by Maalik in AI-Muwatta.

Garaded Da'eef by Al-Al baa nee in Da'eef At-Targheeb wa At-Tarheeb. 71 of which is giving gifts and spreading the greeting of Salaam, because the Prophet ~ said: "You will not enter Paradise until you believe, and you will not believe until you love one another. Shall I not tell you of that which will strengthen love between you? Spread (the greeting of) Salaam amongst yourse/ves."f_79 l Making True and Sincere Repentance from Sins As mentioned earlier, Hasad comes from an unhealthy heart, which perhaps was made dead by sins, no matter how small they may be. Abu Hurayrah ~ narrated that the Messenger of Allaah ~ said: "When a slave of Allaah commits a sin, a black stain is dotted on his heart. Then if that person gives up that sin, begs Allaah to forgive him and repents, then his heart is cleared; but if he repeats the evil deed, then that covering is increased till his heart is completely covered with it.

And that is Ar-Ran, which (79] Muslim. Allaah mentions in the Qur'aan: "Nay! But on their hearts is the Ran (covering of sins and evil deeds), which they used to earn." [AI-Mutaffifeen (83): 14](80] By repenting from these sins and turning to Allaah, the evil of this destructive hatred will lnsha Allaah be removed from your heart. (80] At-Trimidhee. 73 The Praiseworthy Envy Envy is of two types; the blameworthy and destructive envy, which is called Hasad in Arabic, and the praiseworthy envy which is called AI-Ghibtah. Everything that has preceded relates to Hasad, the blameworthy envy.

On the other hand, AI-Ghibtah is to envy the righteous folks and to wish that you also possess the capability to do as much good as they are doing without any form of jealousy and without wishing that the blessings they had been endowed with from their Lord be withdrawn or that it should vanish. This is a good and praiseworthy thing. It therefore means that mutual rivalry and competition in performing acts of righteousness and getting closer to Allaah ~ is a form of good envy. Allaah ~ says: "So hasten! All of you towards all that is good." [AI-Baqarah (2): 148] Competing to excel one another in good deeds should be because of the rewards of the hereafter.

Allaah ~ says: "And for this (reward of Paradise) let all those strive who want to strive (i.e. hasten earnestly to the obedience of Allaah).'' [AI-Mutaffifeen {83): 26] And also He ~ says: "And hasten to the forgiveness from your Lord, and to Paradise as wide as are the heavens and the earth, prepared for the pious who are obedient to Allaah." [AI-Maa'idah (3): 133] This rivalry should not be to accumulate the things of this life, as was the case with the people who envied Qaarun - the arrogant but wealthy man who lived during the time of Prophet Muusaa ~I. Allaah ~ says: , ,-. ,:: ~. 2-H &,yt ~~ ~ --...;...> /lb ~p ~I} ,- )- / 7 / ,,,..,.... ,'// ,- .> ,- ), ~ ~ , ...J (.)~ > .,,,.>~,.,>.-> // ,- J::J J\! °':-,.io J_;i\\ ~j ,,,,,~. ;:.it ~ J-''-~ ~~\ (.)t t ...) ),'' // ~I ~ ;.. ,, ,, !,, ,, ,,- ,,,, ,,,,,.,,,,,,,,,.,,,,,. ~ ........,.; & ~ 0 / .> )- ~\ --...;_.).J..A>....r.. .,, 0.:J1 .,, J\; ..i.:::,., . L_,,,,.,... .,.--}, // ~:~~ £;. ,:, ,,.~, )- >-:: _,.,,. .Jil .,A...:.J~ 0.J.P <::,J..JI C ~ { /, u4 / ,,..,,.. (L:uf ~' ~9 ~{::.~_; ri~1 ~;i ~J, Jt:J__, o (0.J~1 ,, ~l 4-'~ 'l_; ~ ~_; G1~ / "Verily, Qaarun was of Musaa's people, but he behaved arrogantly towards them. And We gave him of the treasures, that of which the keys would have been a burden to a body of strong men.

When his people said to him: 'Do not be glad with ungratefulness to Allaah's Favours. Verily! Allaah likes not those who are glad with ungratefulness to Allaah's Favours. But seek, with that wealth which Allaah has bestowed on you, the home of the Hereafter, and forget not your portion of legal enjoyment in this world, and do good as Allah has been good to you, and seek not mischief in the land. Verily, Allah likes not those who commit great crimes and sins."' He said: "This has been given to me only because of knowledge I possess." 77 Then, Allaah ~ says: / .,, / )- / J. /_ ..> J.,,,,.. { -...... / , // / / / o 1"")~c. +;- _J. :u t'-j! ~up q_JI ~ ~ U ~ c,; 5:.J;.: ,r;r ..-;,Q' 1;{::~_; Fi~rr r; J 0}( Jt:1_; "...

So he went forth before his people in his pomp. Those who were desirous of the life of the world, said: "Ah, would that we had the like of what Qaarun has been given? Verily! He is the owner of a great fortune.'' But those who had been given (religious) knowledge said: "Woe to you! The Reward of Allaah (in the Hereafter) is better for those who believe and do righteous good deeds, and this none shall attain except those who are patient (in following the truth).'' [A/-Qasas (28): 76-80] Due to this, envy is permissible concerning three people as Abdullah bin Mas'ud ~ narrated that : I heard t he Prophet ~ saying: ~ 1;:: ~ j 'i c;, ~ I ~ ti u- ~ 1.::. _)/ : ~ · ::~~ I c.$-· '-1 ~I i.:.;.

'J / ~ / I -:: , ~ _ J ~~ ~ I'.'.'/ I -:: . . ~/ / , -; ~ ~ \ ~ / ti c..r-.J_J ~ l.::. / / ' ~~ _II . ~ ~<-I // " There is no envy except in two: a person whom Allaah has given wealth and he spends it in the right way, and a person 7 whom Allaah has given wisdom (i.e. religious knowledge) and he gives his decisions accordingly and teaches it to the others. "f_s1l And in another narration, the Messenger of Allaah ~ said: "There can be no envy except in two cases: A .man whom Allaah has taught the Qur'aan, and so he recites it during the hours of the night and during the hours of the day, and his neighbour listens to him and says; 'I wish I had been given what has been given to so-and-so, for then I would do what he does; and secondly, a man whom Allaah has given wealth, and thus, he spends it on what is just and right, whereupon another man says; 'I wish I had been given what so-and-so \ has been given, for then I would do the same as what he does." [s 2J [81] AI-Bukhaaree. [82] AI-Bukhaaree. 79 Be Free of Envy ... Gain Allaah's Paradise If you strive as hard as you can to free yourself from the evil of envy, and may Allaah bless your effort, His Paradise will be the reward for you. Surely Allaah's love and His Great pleasure is upon those who struggle to attain the good end. Allaah ~ says about the supplication of believers for their righteous predecessors thus: "And those who came after them say: 'Our Lord!

Forgive us and our brethren who have preceded us in Faith, and put not in our hearts any hatred against those who have believed. Our Lord! You are indeed full of kindness, Most Merciful.'" [AI-Hashr {59): 10] Paradise is a place where there will be no rancour, jealousy or envy. Such bad traits will be removed from the hearts of the believers before they are admitted into AI-Jannah {Paradise). Allaah ~ says: "But those who believed (in the Oneness of Allah - Islamic Monotheism), and worked righteousness - We tax not any person beyond his scope, such are the dwellers of Paradise.

They will abide therein. And We shall remove from their breasts any (mutual) hatred or sense of injury (which they had, if at all, in the life of this world); rivers flowing under them, and they will say: 'All the praises and thanks be to Allah, Who has guided us to this, never could we have found guidance, were it not that Allah had guided us! Indeed, the Messengers of our Lord did come with the truth.' And it will be cried out to them: 'This is the Paradise which you have inherited for what you used to do."' [AI-A'raaf (7): 42-43] Try your best to rid your heart of rancour, envy and hatred against fellow Muslims. Allaah is ready to assist and guide you to success in your efforts. He ~ says: A~\ :J ~ l/ h>>., ~>~-- I::, Li i > " ," ,: ~\"l.- ~\ u,,J '.,,,, C . ~-;-r- .,, :.J~ u-3,, :.J"f' "As for those who strive hard in Us (Our Cause), We will surely guide them to Our Paths (i.e.

Allah's Religion - Islamic Monotheism). And verily, Allah is with the Muhsinun (good doers)." [AI-Ankaboot {29): 69] Beware of being envious of people because it destroys one's religion. Is there any chance of being admitted to Paradise once the religion is destroyed? The Messenger of Allaah ~ said: "You have been afflicted with the illness of the nations that came before you - jealousy and hatred. They are the shearers, I do not mean the shearers of the hair, rather they are the shearers of the religion."l83 l Eemaan is the foundation of the Deen and the main condition for admission into AI-Jannah.

Beware of losing it to envy. The Messenger of Allaah ~ said: '... And belief and envy do not occur together in the heart of a believer."[s4J [83] Saheeh At-Targheeb wa At-Tarheeb [84] An-Nasaa'ee (Hasan) . May Allaah ~ keep our hearts free of the disease of envy. And praise is due to Allaah ~ in the end as it is in the beginning.

May the peace and blessing of Allaah be upon Muhammad ~ , his household and those upon his path till the Day of Resurrection . May Allaah ~ keep our hearts free of the disease of envy. And praise is due to Allaah ~ in the end as it is in the beginning. May the peace and blessing of Allaah be upon Muhammad ~ , his household and those upon his path till the Day of Resurrection. And Allaah is our Source of help! ...

End ... Index of Hadeeths Al-'ayn could lead a man to his grave, and a camel to the cooking pot 38 Al-'ayn is real; if anything were to compete with the Qadar 38 Alfaah will forgive my Ummah for their mistakes, what they forget 54 And belief and envy do not occur together in the heart of a believer 62 Creeping and advancing upon you is the disease and malady 16 Creeping and advancing upon you is the disease and malady of 35 Do not be envious of one another and do not nurse 13 Do not make me a witness for injustice 30 Guide my heart and remove ill will from my breast 52 He has indeed asked Alfaah and invoked Him by virtue of His 48 He who has in his heart the weight of a mustard seed of pride 28 How would you be, 0 people, when Persia (Iran) and Rome 24 I seek refuge for you both in the perfect words of Af/aah 40 I seek refuge in the perfect words of Alfaah from His wrath 42 I seek refuge in the perfect words of Alfaah from the evil that 40 I seek refuge in the perfect words of Alfaah, through which no man 41 I seek refuge with Alfaah from every devil and poisonous reptile 41 I will pave the way for you as your predecessor and will be a witness 25 In the name of Alfaah I pray for you, from everything that may 43 Indeed, you have asked Alfaiinthe Most Exalted by the Names 48 Look at those who are lower than you but do not look at those 24 No human is killed or murdered unjustly, but a part of responsibility 22 No, pride is disdaining what is true and despising people 28 None of you would have believed until he loves for his brother 26 Nothing else besides it? You would in fact vie with one another 25 0 Alfaah ! I seek refuge with Your Perfect Words from every devil 40 85 0 Allaah, You are my Lord, there is no god besides You 42 Recite Ruqyah for her, because she is affected by a look (Nazrah) 37 Shake hands, for this will dispel rancour, and exchange gifts 55 Strange and amazing are the ways and affairs of a believer 37 Surely, you have asked Allaah by virtue of His greatest Names 48 Tell Abu Thaabit to seek refuge with Allaah 39 The best day on which the sun has risen is Friday 50 The following two persons do not exist together in hell 27 The religion is Naseehah (sincere advice) 10 There can be no envy except in two cases: A man whom Allaah 59 There is no envy except in two: a person whom Allaah has given 59 Verily the most truthful speech is the Word of Allaah and the 8 Verily! In the body is a morsel of flesh which, if it is rectified 26 Verily!

The lawful is clear and the unlawful is clear 26 Verily, Allaah is Graceful and He loves Grace 28 What Allaah wills, and there is no strength except in Allaah 43 When a man has two wives and he is inclined to one of them 30 When a slave of Allaah commits a sin, a black stain is dotted 55 Why would any one of you kill his brother? If any one of you sees 32 Why would any one of you kill his brother? If any one of you sees 36 Why would any one of you kill his brother? If any one of you sees 45 Woe to you, you have cut off your companion's neck 32 Yes, for if anything were to compete with Allaah's decree (Al-qadar) 39 You have been afflicted with the illness of the nations that came before 62 You will not enter Paradise until you believe, and you will not 54 Your forefather (i.e. lbraaheem iW,) used to seek refuge with Allaah 40 Obstacles on the path of DawahDr. Sayed Noah AI-Mansurah, Egypt: Darul-Yaqeen 1421 A.H (2000) Commentary on the Forty Hadith of An-Nawawi - Jamal-Deen M.

content_id:
Cara lain menghadapi al-'ayn adalah mencoba untuk tidak memicunya sejak awal, dengan menutupi keindahan orang yang ditakutkan terkena dampaknya dengan sesuatu yang dapat menolaknya. 'Utsman (ra) melihat seorang anak laki-laki tampan, dan berkata: "Beri tanda hitam di dagunya, agar al-'ayn tidak mencelakakannya" — maksudnya agar mereka memberi sesuatu yang berwarna hitam di lekukan dagunya.

Syaikh Muhammad as-Shalih bin Uthaymeen berkata: "Sebagian dari pengobatan pengaruh pandangan jahat adalah meminta orang yang menyebabkan pandangan jahat kepada orang lain untuk mandi, sebagaimana Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan 'Amir bin Rabi'ah (ra) untuk melakukan hal itu dalam hadis yang disebutkan di atas. Kemudian air tersebut disiramkan ke atas orang yang terkena dampak. Adapun mengambil kotorannya, seperti air kencingnya dan tinjanya, maka tidak ada dasarnya; hal yang sama berlaku untuk mengambil barang-barang miliknya. Melainkan yang diriwayatkan adalah seperti yang disebutkan di atas, membasuh anggota tubuhnya dan bagian dalam pakaiannya, atau demikian pula membasuh bagian dalam serban dan pakaiannya. Dan Allah lebih mengetahui. Tidak ada yang salah dengan mengambil tindakan pencegahan terhadap pandangan jahat sebelum hal itu terjadi, dan ini tidak bertentangan dengan gagasan tawakkal (berserah diri kepada Allah). Bahkan ini adalah tawakkal, karena tawakkal berarti berserah kepada Allah sambil juga menerapkan cara-cara yang telah diizinkan atau diwajibkan..."

Allah ﷻ memuliakan semua ciptaan-Nya sesuai yang Ia kehendaki. Mengapa kamu harus hasad kepada makhluk lain sepertimu, sementara Allahlah yang memberikan keutamaan kepada siapa saja di antara mereka sesuai yang Ia kehendaki berdasarkan Hikmah dan Penilaian-Nya yang Sempurna. Inilah Sunnah Allah dan tidak ada perubahan padanya.

Allah ﷻ memberikan keutamaan pada berbagai hal dalam ciptaan-Nya: pada Nama-nama-Nya (yang terbesar), pada Kitab-Kitab-Nya (Al-Qur'an yang paling utama), pada para Nabi-Nya (Muhammad ﷺ yang paling mulia), pada umat Islam (Abu Bakar ash-Shiddiq (ra) yang terbaik setelah Nabi ﷺ), pada anak cucu Adam atas seluruh ciptaan, serta pada hari dan malam (Lailatul Qadar yang lebih baik dari seribu bulan, dan Jumat sebagai hari terbaik dalam sepekan).

Jika kamu merenungkan semua yang telah disebutkan, kamu akan melihat bahwa Allah adalah Pelaku dari semua yang Ia kehendaki dan tidak ada yang dapat mempertanyakan Otoritas-Nya. Berbagai bentuk keutamaan yang diberikan kepada orang-orang yang berbeda seharusnya menanamkan dalam dirimu: (a) keimanan mutlak kepada Allah; (b) ridha terhadap takdir sambil terus berusaha; (c) kemampuan untuk menghindari persaingan demi keunggulan dunia ini.

Di antara cara-cara pengobatan hasad yang lain adalah: saling mencintai (mencintai untuk saudaramu apa yang kamu cintai untuk dirimu sendiri), bertakwa dan bersabar, berdoa memohon kepada Allah agar terbebas dari hasad, membaca Al-Qur'an dan merenungkan maknanya, membaca sirah Nabi ﷺ dan para Salafus-Shalih, berlindung kepada Allah dari was-was Setan, memberikan hadiah dan berjabat tangan, mengucapkan salam, serta bertaubat dengan sungguh-sungguh dari dosa-dosa.

Hasad terbagi menjadi dua jenis: hasad yang tercela dan merusak yang disebut hasad dalam bahasa Arab, dan hasad yang terpuji yang disebut al-ghibtah. Al-ghibtah adalah mengiri orang-orang saleh dan mengharapkan agar kamu juga memiliki kemampuan untuk melakukan kebaikan sebanyak yang mereka lakukan tanpa rasa dengki dan tanpa mengharapkan nikmat yang mereka miliki dicabut. Ini adalah hal yang baik dan terpuji. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Tidak ada hasad kecuali dalam dua hal: seseorang yang Allah berikan harta dan ia membelanjakannya di jalan yang benar, dan seseorang yang Allah berikan hikmah (ilmu agama) dan ia memutuskan berdasarkannya serta mengajarkannya kepada orang lain." [HR. Bukhari]

Jika kamu berusaha sekuat tenaga untuk membebaskan dirimu dari kejahatan hasad, surga Allah akan menjadi balasanmu. Hadis Nabi ﷺ mengingatkan: "...Dan iman dan hasad tidak bisa berkumpul bersama dalam hati seorang mukmin." Semoga Allah ﷻ menjaga hati kita dari penyakit hasad. Segala puji bagi Allah ﷻ di akhir sebagaimana di awal. Semoga shalawat dan salam Allah tercurah kepada Muhammad ﷺ, keluarganya, dan mereka yang mengikuti jalannya hingga Hari Kebangkitan.
content_ar: Another الطريق of dealing with Al-'Ayn is to try not to provoke it in the أولاً place, by covering the beauty of the one for whom you الخوف with something that will repel it. 'Uthmaan ~ saw a handsome boy, and said : "Put something black on his chin, lest Al-'Ayn harm him" - meaning that they should put something black in the dimple of his chin. r631 (62] الموطأ lmaam مالك. Shaykh محمد ﷺ As-Saalih bin Uthaymeen said: "Part of the treatment of the الشر eye effect is asking the person who has put the الشر eye on another to wash, as النبي ﷺ ~ commanded 'Aamir bin Rabee'ah ~ to do in the الحديث quoted above. Then the water should be poured over the one who has been afflicted . فيما يتعلق بـ taking his waste, مثل his urine and stools, there is no basis for doing لذا; the same applies to taking any of his belongings. Rather what is narrated is that which is mentioned above, washing his limbs and washing inside his garment, or likewise washing inside his headgear and garments. And Allaah knows best. There is nothing الخطأ with taking precautions against the الشر eye قبل it happens, and this does not contradict the idea of التوكل (putting one's trust in Allaah) . In fact this is التوكل, لأن التوكل means putting one's t rust in Allaah whilst أيضاً implementing the means that have been permitted or enjoined ... " (AI-Fataawa) (63] AI-Baghawi - Sharh AI-السنة: 59 Treatment for الحسد (A general guide) Allaah ~ grades all of His creation the الطريق He likes Why should you be envious of another creature like yourself بينما it is Allaah ~ that grants excellence to any amongst them the الطريق He desires based upon His Perfect الحكمة and Judgement. This is the السنة of Allaah and in it there is no alteration. He ~ says: ~,,,, / ,,,,,,, , , . , , ~-:F >t ,,,,,,,... J// ..., , , , , , , , , ., ,. ., , ,. " " , , ., , // / ~ ~~~ ~I ~ ~ . ~ ~ .r,_ c.1;. ~ ~ I ~I / / ~ r "That has been the الطريق of Allaah already with those who passed away قبل. And you will not find any change in the الطريق of Allaah." [Al-Fath (48): 23] لذا you will find that Allaah ~ has given an edge to some over the others even among His Names! Excellence among His Names and Attributes All of Allaah's Names and Attributes are perfect and beautiful. غير أن, some amongst them have been labelled the "greatest names by His Messenger ~ - Buraydah ~ narrated that الرسول ﷺ of Allaah heard a man praying and he was saying: "O Allaah ! Verily I ask you by الفضيلة of the fact that I testify that you are Allaah and there is no deity worthy of worship except you; AI-AhacJf-641, As-SamacJf65 1, He who does not beget nor was He begotten and there is no one comparable to Him." Then الرسول ﷺ of Allaah :i said : / ,,,, ,., ~ J ~G:-1 ~ ~~ "Indeed, you have asked Allaah the Most Exalted by the Names with which عندما He is asked, He gives, and عندما He is invoked He responds. " And in another narration, قال: _1;.;.\11 /.&1 t"""• ~:- 4111 ~L .Ll r- / / "Surely, you have asked Allaah by الفضيلة of His greatest Names."f-661 [64] The Only One. (65] The Independent, the Eternal and the Absolute. (66] أبو داود, Tirmidhee and An-Nasaaee. Excellence among His الكتب Allaah has revealed الكتب to His الأنبياء and الرسل. غير أن, He made the Qur'aan to excel all of them and He ~ has promised to protect it from any alteration. A promise not given to the earlier الكتب. He ~ says: "Verily It is We who have sent down the الذكر (i.e. the Qur'aan) and surely, We will guard it from corruption." [AIHijr (15) : 9] Excellence amongst His الأنبياء Allaah ~ compared the people for excellence, the best of them being the الأنبياء, and He ~ compared the الرسل for excellence, the most excellent of them being محمد ﷺ ~ - Allaah ~ says: At a different time, أنس bin مالك ..t;, narrated that بينما he was seated with الرسول ﷺ of Allaah ~ and a man was offering the Sa/aah and thereafter he prayed saying: "O Allaah ! Certainly I ask you by الفضيلة of the fact that the Praise is for you, there is no deity worthy of worship in الحق except you; Al - Manaan - The Benefactor, Badee'u samaawaati wal-ard - The Originator of the Heavens and the earth, Ya Dha/-Jalaal wal-/kraam - Owner of Majesty and Honour, Yaa Hayyu Yaa Qayyuum - The Ever living one that dies not, The Self-Subsisting and Independent." لذا النبي ﷺ ~ said : "He has indeed asked Allaah and invoked Him by الفضيلة of His greatest Names, with which كلما He is invoked He responds and عندما He is asked by them, He gives." (أبو داود and An-Nasaa'ee.) 63 "Those الرسل! We preferred some to others." [AIBaqarah (2): 253]. Excellence among the مسلم Nation Allaah g compared the المؤمنون in the الأمة (الجماعة) of محمد ﷺ ~ for excellence, and the best of them, بعد His النبي ti was أبو بكر As-Siddeeq ~ .[671 Excellence of the الأولاد of Aadam over the rest of creation: Allaah ~ says: "And indeed we have honoured the الأولاد of Aadam, and we have carried them on land and sea, and have provided them with lawful الخير things, and have preferred them above many of those whom we have created with a marked preference." [Al-lsraa (17): 70] Allaah ~ compared the الأولاد of Aadam for excellence and (67) Narrated محمد ﷺ bin Al Hanafiya I asked my الأب ('Alee bin Abi Taalib ~ ), "Who are the best people بعد Allaah's Messenger ~ ?" قال, "أبو بكر." I asked, "Who then?" قال, "Then 'عمر." I was afraid he would say "عثمان," لذا I said, "Then you?" قال, "I am only an ordinary person ." (AI-Bukhaaree) Likewise lbn عمر ~ narrated that: "We used to compare the people as to who was better خلال the lifetime of Allaah's Messenger ~ - We used to regard أبو بكر as the best, then 'عمر, and then 'عثمان." (AI-Bukhaaree) 64 made the people of goodness and benefits to mankind excel the others. He ~ says: ,\; _) >~- " ~ ~'_,, t ~~ ~,_,.> J":, )l::.A /// >-;; \ , u ' / / }.._ i;_r _ l 1..--- -: .i..ll Y~_J "T J:oi ~ -t '1 i.p;> <>" ,/ J ~ ..r-- .:-:>: d - LllJ-4 ;., '-::-' - ~~ '~·__,, ~ ,I._ -~'~.1,: ,'(; J1Jt;., _r4 "'Z -1 -~ ..... /J"f'; &-' Y'-' / '! .>>t _,,,, ,,, . / > / - (Y_, Y' , :'" l>r "And Allaah puts forward another example of two men, one of them dumb, who has no power over anything (disbeliever) , and he is a burden to his master, whichever الطريق he directs him, he brings no الخير. Is such a man equal to one (المؤمن) who commands العدل, and is himself on the Straight الطريق?" [An-Nahl (16): 76]. He ~ even assigned excellence amongst the foods and plants: "And in the earth are neighbouring tracts and gardens of vines, and fields sown with corn, and palm trees, growing out two or three from a single stem root, or otherwise (one stem for every palm), watered with the same water, yet some of them We make more excellent than others to eat. Verily, in these things, there are signs for the people who understand." [Ar-Ra'd (13): 4]. 65 Excellence of the Night and Day: Allaah ~ compared the days on earth for excellence, Layla-tu/-Qadr - The Night of Decree - being better than a thousand months: He ~ says: "Layla-tul-Qadr is better than a thousand months." [AIQadr (97): 3]. And He ~ made Yawmul الجمعة - Friday - to excel the rest of the days of the week. Narrated أبو هريرة ~ - "Allaah's Messenger ~ said: 4-' ;T /-\~ W 4 ~ \ -'o/ >~{I\~ ~ i . ~ /-/ / . i y.. ~ /- i y..o/ ~ >o-; r:; ., ,.,., • .,.,.... ,,,, ., ,,,,.,, ~ 'll ~w1 f.;; '1:, 4~ t_/i ~:, 4~1 ~;i ~:, 0 ., ~ \ 0 / • / . iY..c.1- "The best day on which the sun has risen is Friday; on it Aadam ~\ was created, on it he was made to enter الجنة, on it he was expelled from it."l 68 l إذا you reflect carefully upon all that has been stated, you will see that Allaah is the Doer of all that He intends and that no one can question His Authority. Instead of grudging Allaah's decision, the various forms of excellence granted to different people should instill in you: a. The absolute الإيمان in Allaah . b. Being pleased with القدر as you continue to strive in your endeavours. [68) مسلم. c. The ability to avoid the rivalry for excellence for the sake of th is الدنيا. This is the أولاً and the surest الدواء for the di sease of الحسد. From the other ways of الدواء of this المرض are: المحبة of بعضهم بعضاً What every مسلم is obliged to do is to المحبة for his الأخ what he loves for himself of الخير things, and to الكراهية for his الأخ what he hates for himself of bad things. This does not mean that you cannot like for yourself what you like for others. إذا you see that your الأخ has something that you do not possess, and wish that you have it أيضاً, this is the الخير الحسد that is free from malicel69l; غير أن, إذا you wish that the البركة would be taken away from him, this is the destructive الغيرة. As a مسلم, you need to strive against your own selfl 70l لذا that your القلب will be free of الغيرة towards your مسلم brothers. إذا you sincerely Jove your brothers, most of these problems from which you might be المعاناة will disappear. [69] This is the praiseworthy الحسد called AI-Ghibtah. A discussion on it follows shortly. [70] Jihaad AI-Nafs: Striving against the النفس لأن it is always inclined to الشر. Allaah ~ says : يوسف ﷺ ~ said : "And I free not myself from the blame. Verily, the human self is inclined to الشر, except عندما my Lord bestows His الرحمة upon whom He wills. Verily, my Lord is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful." [يوسف ﷺ (12) : 53] 67 عندما you realize how great your الفضيلة and status will be عندما you المحبة your brothers and المحبة الخير things for them, and عندما you know how great your الأجر will be إذا you treat them well, this will motivate you to treat them well in all ways, and to strive to benefit your brothers and sisters instead of being preoccupied with jealous thoughts of what they have and you do not have. You need to reflect and think deeply these words of Allaah: 1': ~ j. ,,, fi. .,,, _,.,:.1.,,, .),..,,,..... ., , / 'J ,-;,, ). ,:, ,-; / ,,,, ~ , ~ t:t'.J JJ!\_J ~~ 0-4 ~J: ~1 µ ~·~r "That (which you are envious and sad about) is the الفضل of Allaah which He bestows on whom He wills. And Allaah is All-Sufficient for His creatures' needs, All-Knower." [AIMad'idah (5) :54] And أيضاً His ~ saying: "Is it they who would portion out the الرحمة of your Lord? It is We Who portion out between them their livelihood in this الدنيا, and We raised some of them above others in ranks, لذا that some may employ others in their work. But the الرحمة {الجنة) of your Lord is better than the الثروة of this الدنيا, which they amass." [Az-Zukhruf (43): 32] Piety and الصبر الحسد - Destructive الغيرة - causes a great deal of harm in this الدنيا and in الآخرة. P 1l That is why "Whoever finds in himself any الحسد towards another has to try to neutralize it by means of piety and consciousness of Allaah along with الصبر. لذا he should الكراهية the feeling of الحسد in himself... But the one who does الخطأ to his الأخ by word or deed will be punished for that. The one who fears Allaah and is patient, غير أن, is not included among the wrongdoers, and Allaah will benefit him by الفضيلة of his piety and consciousness of Allaah ." (721 الدعاء Make Du'aa' (الدعاء) to Allaah ~ and ask Him to rid you of th is problem. النبي ﷺ ~ used to say in his Du'aa', "Guide my القلب and remove ill will from my breast."f.731 Reading the Qur'aan. Ponderingthe meanings of the Qur'aan and reading it frequently, ولا سيما the الآيات which speak of الحسد (destructive الغيرة). Reading the Qur'aan brings you a great deal of الأجر for الخير (71] This is evident from the الحديث of AI-Zubayr bin AI-Awwam ~ stated on page 36. (72] Shaykh ul-lslaam lbn Taymiyyah : Amraad AI-Qu/oob (Diseases of the القلب) : (73] At-Tirmidhee. J o ,.,. l I ·- 1/ • I/ O ,,,, O / •/ . / I; (.>.)...L..., .. ,,,, ~ r...r_J (J':"'"' .,. ,,,, ~ _J " Wa 'hdi qa/bi wa'slul sakheemata sadree'~ The phrase "guide my القلب" means to the straight الطريق, and "remove ill will from my breast" means take away all insincerity, rancour and hatred. deeds. Allaah ~ says: "Verily, the الخير deeds remove the الشر deeds.'' [Hud (11):114] And certainly, it is in the words of Allaah that all القلوب find rest and السكينة. Allaah ~ says: ~yfll ~ .&, ~~ 1t~ !::,, / ,,.,,. /;,, "Verily, in the الذكر of Allaah do القلوب find rest.'' [Ar-Ra'd (13): 28] Reading the History of النبي ﷺ and the Salafus-Saaliheen[74J Reading the SeeraH 15 l of الرسول ﷺ of Allaah ~ , seeing how he kept away from الحسد and how he loved الخير for others, even for his enemies. Seeking Allaah's refuge! إذا any such thoughts of الحسد, cross your العقل, then seek refuge with Allaah from the accursed Shaytaan, and keep yourself (74] Those consisting of the الصحابة, their التابعين and their الصالح followers and upholders of the السنة from the earlier generations of المسلمون. (75] Biography. An addition to this is reading the biographies and stories of the Sahaabah. busy with something that will make you forget these insinuating whispers and thoughts. إذا the Shaytaan manages to instil الحسد in your القلب, then beware lest you say or do anything, which will show that الحسد. Every person has his or her share of الحسد. Shaykh Al-lslaam lbn Taymiyyah said: "Nobody is free from الحسد, but the noble person hides it whilst the base person shows it."[76l Remember, a person will not be brought to account for whatever crosses his العقل, but he will be brought to account for what he says and does. النبي ﷺ ~ said: <~· I t; J/ 0r ~ I.r' ..r"-'-'-" / M .,,, / ·~11 J/ /tl;;J1 ~ M ·/ ~ / ~ ,,,, . -"½ / ., ... "Allaah will forgive my Um mah for their mistakes, what they forget and what they are forced to do."f.771 Give a الهبة and a handshake. إذا you feel that you are jealous of a specific person, then buy him a الهبة and shake hands with him. النبي ﷺ ~ said : is:.ll101.:; 1;~ '1~;~_:; 'JJ1 0~ ' I ~ ~ "Shake hands, for this will dispel rancour, and exchange gifts and المحبة بعضهم بعضاً, for this will dispel hatred."17 81 Saying Salaam Alaykum! الحسد is the result of hatred, whose opposite is المحبة, the الطريق [76] Amraad AI-Quloob - Diseases of the القلوب. [77] AI-Bukhaaree. [78] Narrated by Maalik in AI-الموطأ. Garaded ضعيف by Al-Al baa nee in ضعيف At-Targheeb wa At-Tarheeb. 71 of which is giving gifts and spreading the greeting of Salaam, لأن النبي ﷺ ~ said: "You will not enter الجنة حتى you believe, and you will not believe حتى you المحبة بعضهم بعضاً. Shall I not tell you of that which will strengthen المحبة between you? Spread (the greeting of) Salaam amongst yourse/ves."f_79 l Making True and Sincere التوبة from الذنوب As mentioned earlier, الحسد comes from an unhealthy القلب, which perhaps was made dead by الذنوب, no matter how small they may be. أبو هريرة ~ narrated that الرسول ﷺ of Allaah ~ said: "عندما a slave of Allaah commits a الذنب, a black stain is dotted on his القلب. Then إذا that person gives up that الذنب, begs Allaah to forgive him and repents, then his القلب is cleared; but إذا he repeats the الشر deed, then that covering is increased till his القلب is completely covered with it. And that is Ar-Ran, which (79] مسلم. Allaah mentions in the Qur'aan: "Nay! But on their القلوب is the Ran (covering of الذنوب and الشر deeds), which they used to earn." [AI-Mutaffifeen (83): 14](80] By repenting from these الذنوب and turning to Allaah, the الشر of this destructive hatred will lnsha Allaah be removed from your القلب. (80] At-Trimidhee. 73 The Praiseworthy الحسد الحسد is of two types; the blameworthy and destructive الحسد, which is called الحسد in Arabic, and the praiseworthy الحسد which is called AI-Ghibtah. Everything that has preceded relates to الحسد, the blameworthy الحسد. من ناحية أخرى, AI-Ghibtah is to الحسد the الصالح folks and to wish that you أيضاً possess the capability to do as much الخير as they are doing without any form of الغيرة and without wishing that the البركات they had been endowed with from their Lord be withdrawn or that it should vanish. This is a الخير and praiseworthy thing. It لذلك means that mutual rivalry and competition in performing acts of righteousness and getting closer to Allaah ~ is a form of الخير الحسد. Allaah ~ says: "لذا hasten! All of you towards all that is الخير." [AI-Baqarah (2): 148] Competing to excel بعضهم بعضاً in الخير deeds should be لأن of the rewards of الآخرة. Allaah ~ says: "And for this (الأجر of الجنة) let all those strive who want to strive (i.e. hasten earnestly to the obedience of Allaah).'' [AI-Mutaffifeen {83): 26] And أيضاً He ~ says: "And hasten to the المغفرة from your Lord, and to الجنة as wide as are the heavens and the earth, prepared for the pious who are obedient to Allaah." [AI-Maa'idah (3): 133] This rivalry should not be to accumulate the things of this الحياة, as was the case with the people who envied Qaarun - the arrogant but wealthy man who lived خلال the time of النبي Muusaa ~I. (L:uf ~' ~9 ~{::.~_; ri~1 ~;i ~J, Jt:J__, o (0.J~1 ,, ~l 4-'~ 'l_; ~ ~_; G1~ / "Verily, Qaarun was of Musaa's people, but he behaved arrogantly towards them. And We gave him of the treasures, that of which the keys would have been a burden to a الجسد of strong men. عندما his people said to him: 'Do not be glad with ungratefulness to Allaah's Favours. Verily! Allaah likes not those who are glad with ungratefulness to Allaah's Favours. But seek, with that الثروة which Allaah has bestowed on you, the home of الآخرة, and forget not your portion of legal enjoyment in this الدنيا, and do الخير as الله has been الخير to you, and seek not mischief in the land. Verily, الله likes not those who commit great crimes and الذنوب."' قال: "This has been given to me only لأن of العلم I possess." 77 Then, Allaah ~ says: / .,, / )- / J. /_ ..> J.,,,,.. { -...... / , // / / / o 1"")~c. +;- _J. :u t'-j! ~up q_JI ~ ~ U ~ c,; 5:.J;.: ,r;r ..-;,Q' 1;{::~_; Fi~rr r; J 0}( Jt:1_; "... لذا he went forth قبل his people in his pomp. Those who were desirous of the الحياة of the الدنيا, said: "Ah, would that we had the like of what Qaarun has been given? Verily! He is the owner of a great fortune.'' But those who had been given (religious) العلم said: "Woe to you! The الأجر of Allaah (in الآخرة) is better for those who believe and do الصالح الخير deeds, and this none shall attain except those who are patient (in following the الحق).'' [A/-Qasas (28): 76-80] بسبب this, الحسد is permissible فيما يتعلق بـ three people as Abdullah bin Mas'ud ~ narrated that : I heard t he النبي ~ saying: ~ 1;:: ~ j 'i c;, ~ I ~ ti u- ~ 1.::. _)/ : ~ · ::~~ I c.$-· '-1 ~I i.:.;. 'J / ~ / I -:: , ~ _ J ~~ ~ I'.'.'/ I -:: . . ~/ / , -; ~ ~ \ ~ / ti c..r-.J_J ~ l.::. / / ' ~~ _II . ~ ~<-I // " There is no الحسد except in two: a person whom Allaah has given الثروة and he spends it in the الحق الطريق, and a person 7 whom Allaah has given الحكمة (i.e. religious العلم) and he gives his decisions وبناءً على ذلك and teaches it to the others. "f_s1l And in another narration, الرسول ﷺ of Allaah ~ said: "There can be no الحسد except in two cases: A .man whom Allaah has taught the Qur'aan, and لذا he recites it خلال the hours of the night and خلال the hours of the day, and his neighbour listens to him and says; 'I wish I had been given what has been given to لذا-and-لذا, for then I would do what he does; and secondly, a man whom Allaah has given الثروة, and وهكذا, he spends it on what is just and الحق, whereupon another man says; 'I wish I had been given what لذا-and-لذا \ has been given, for then I would do the same as what he does." [s 2J [81] AI-Bukhaaree. [82] AI-Bukhaaree. 79 Be Free of الحسد ... Gain Allaah's الجنة إذا you strive as hard as you can to free yourself from the الشر of الحسد, and may Allaah bless your effort, His الجنة will be the الأجر for you. Surely Allaah's المحبة and His Great pleasure is upon those who struggle to attain the الخير end. Allaah ~ says about the الدعاء of المؤمنون for their الصالح predecessors وهكذا: "And those who came بعد them say: 'Our Lord! Forgive us and our brethren who have preceded us in الإيمان, and put not in our القلوب any hatred against those who have believed. Our Lord! You are indeed full of اللطف, Most Merciful.'" [AI-Hashr {59): 10] الجنة is a place where there will be no rancour, الغيرة or الحسد. Such bad traits will be removed from the القلوب of the المؤمنون قبل they are admitted into AI-Jannah {الجنة). Allaah ~ says: "But those who believed (in the Oneness of الله - Islamic Monotheism), and worked righteousness - We tax not any person beyond his scope, such are the dwellers of الجنة. They will abide therein. And We shall remove from their breasts any (mutual) hatred or sense of injury (which they had, إذا at all, in the الحياة of this الدنيا); rivers flowing under them, and they will say: 'All the praises and thanks be to الله, Who has guided us to this, never could we have found الهداية, were it not that الله had guided us! Indeed, the الرسل of our Lord did come with the الحق.' And it will be cried out to them: 'This is the الجنة which you have inherited for what you used to do."' [AI-A'raaf (7): 42-43] Try your best to rid your القلب of rancour, الحسد and hatred against fellow المسلمون. Allaah is ready to assist and guide you to الفلاح in your efforts. He ~ says: A~\ :J ~ l/ h>>., ~>~-- I::, Li i > " ," ,: ~\"l.- ~\ u,,J '.,,,, C . ~-;-r- .,, :.J~ u-3,, :.J"f' "أما those who strive hard in Us (Our Cause), We will surely guide them to Our Paths (i.e. الله's Religion - Islamic Monotheism). And verily, الله is with the Muhsinun (الخير doers)." [AI-Ankaboot {29): 69] Beware of being envious of people لأن it destroys one's religion. Is there any chance of being admitted to الجنة حين the religion is destroyed? الرسول ﷺ of Allaah ~ said: "You have been afflicted with the المرض of the nations that came قبل you - الغيرة and hatred. They are the shearers, I do not mean the shearers of the hair, rather they are the shearers of the religion."l83 l Eemaan is the foundation of the Deen and the الرئيسي condition for admission into AI-Jannah. Beware of losing it to الحسد. الرسول ﷺ of Allaah ~ said: '... And الاعتقاد and الحسد do not occur together in the القلب of a المؤمن."[s4J [83] Saheeh At-Targheeb wa At-Tarheeb [84] An-Nasaa'ee (حسن) . May Allaah ~ keep our القلوب free of the المرض of الحسد. And praise is بسبب Allaah ~ in the end as it is in the beginning. May the peace and البركة of Allaah be upon محمد ﷺ ~ , his household and those upon his الطريق till the Day of البعث . May Allaah ~ keep our القلوب free of the المرض of الحسد. And praise is بسبب Allaah ~ in the end as it is in the beginning. May the peace and البركة of Allaah be upon محمد ﷺ ~ , his household and those upon his الطريق till the Day of البعث. And Allaah is our Source of help! ... End ...
